mur_pierre.glb deployed on all 3 walls. LairGLBAssets.ts lines 77-96: back wall, left wall (rotated PI/2), right wall (rotated -PI/2) all loaded from same GLB. Procedural wallsGroup hidden on success (line 95).

bugs.title: mur_pierre.glb scale produces non-uniform stone stretching
bugs.detail: mur_pierre.glb is authored as a '5 rows × 4 stones = 126 poly' tile (lair_assets_report.json line 41). The Blender script targets a natural tile of approximately 4 wide × 5 tall units (stone aspect ratio ~1:1). Applied scales: back=24×16, sides=20×16 (LairGLBAssets.ts lines 80, 85, 91). At scale.x=24 on a 4-unit-wide tile, each stone is stretched 6× horizontally. At scale.y=16 on a 5-unit-tall tile, each stone is stretched 3.2× vertically. Net horizontal:vertical distortion ratio is 6÷3.2 = ~1.87. Result: every stone becomes a wide flat slab (roughly 2:1 width-to-height instead of 1:1). This is below the AAA distortion threshold of 1.3. The wall will read as horizontal bands rather than stone masonry. Severity is HIGH, not CRITICAL, because: (a) flat shading masks subtle UV stretching, (b) the camera is first-person at ~1.7 units height looking at a 16-unit wall — peripheral distortion is less salient than center-frame. However, the effect is clearly visible on the back wall which is always in frame.
bugs.recommendation: Two options: (A) Re-author mur_pierre.glb Blender script to produce a 24-wide × 16-tall tile at 1:1 stone aspect (4 cols × 5 rows = 20 stones, ~140 polys — within budget). (B) Keep current GLB and tile it: load the GLB once, then clone it 6 times in a 6×1 grid for back wall (each instance scale.x=4, scale.y=16) rather than a single stretched instance. Option B is zero-Blender and achieves correct stone aspect ratio with the existing asset.

bugs.title: Cauldron z-fighting: procedural cauldron NOT explicitly hidden on GLB load success
bugs.detail: LairGLBAssets.ts lines 26-37: cauldron_merlin.glb loads and adds gltf.scene to the scene. However, there is NO reference to a procedural cauldron mesh in LairProceduralGroups (interface at lines 14-19 lists only mapGroup, shelfGroup, floorMesh, wallsGroup — no cauldronGroup). The procedural cauldron created inside MerlinLairScene.ts is added directly to the scene and is never hidden when the GLB loads. lair_assets_report.json note line 43 confirms: 'GLB load is fire-and-forget with .catch() fallback — procedural cauldron remains if GLB fails' — this is the failure path, but because there is no hide call on the success path either, both meshes coexist at the same world position. Z-fighting will occur at (2, -4.65, -7) between the GLB (scale 0.72) and whatever radius the procedural cauldron occupies.
bugs.recommendation: Add cauldronGroup to LairProceduralGroups interface and pass it through loadLairGLBs(), then add 'if (proceduralGroups?.cauldronGroup) proceduralGroups.cauldronGroup.visible = false;' inside the cauldron .then() callback — same pattern as mapGroup (line 54) and shelfGroup (line 62).

bugs.title: bougie.glb clone(true) does not call material.clone() — shared material reference
bugs.detail: LairGLBAssets.ts line 42: 'const clone = gltf.scene.clone(true)'. THREE.Object3D.clone(true) deep-clones the geometry but does NOT clone materials — all three candle instances share the same material reference. This is benign now (no per-candle animation), but any future candle flicker (emissive intensity modulation or color shift) applied to one Material instance will affect all three candles simultaneously. This breaks the independent-flicker illusion.
bugs.recommendation: After clone, traverse each cloned bougie and call material.clone() on every Mesh child: 'clone.traverse(child => { if (child instanceof THREE.Mesh) child.material = (child.material as THREE.Material).clone(); })'. Flag as pre-condition for T-BOUGIE-FLICKER task, not a blocker for C42.

bugs.title: Zone sublabels 'Bientôt disponible' break Celtic immersion
bugs.detail: main.ts lines 186-189: four zones (map, crystal, bookshelf, cauldron) share the identical sublabel 'Bientôt disponible'. This is a generic product placeholder in French tech jargon with zero lore register. In a demo context where first impressions define retention, repetition of the same string four times additionally signals an unfinished product rather than a mysterious sealed world. Immersion rubric score: 3/10 (generic, repeated, no in-world voice).
bugs.recommendation: Replace with distinct Celtic lore-flavored sublabels (see immersion_recommendations section below). Cost: 4 string literals, zero runtime impact.

map.suggested: Les chemins se révèlent en marchant
map.rationale: Druidic principle of experiential knowledge — the map reveals itself through play, not theory. Avoids 'coming soon' register.

crystal.suggested: Les runes ne répondent pas encore
crystal.rationale: Ogham-flavored. Implies latent power awaiting activation. Consistent with the Ogham unlock progression system.

bookshelf.suggested: Les pages sont encore en gestation
bookshelf.rationale: Bardic / druidic oral-to-written metaphor. Journal de Merlin is unwritten until runs happen — lore-coherent.

cauldron.rationale: Directly references the cross-run Anam mechanic from GDB v2.4. Rewards players who read lore. Cauldron as soul-vessel is architecturally consistent.

score_justification.visual_fidelity: 7.5/10 — Three walls now covered by mur_pierre.glb (significant improvement). Deducted 2.5 for BUG-L-SCALE-01: stone distortion ratio ~1.87 is visually degrading on the back wall. flatShading on Skull/PotionBottles confirms house style compliance.
score_justification.immersion_lore: 5.5/10 — Door label restored (+0.5). Four zones with identical 'Bientôt disponible' (-1.5). No Celtic voice in any sublabel. Would score 8.0 with recommended strings applied.
score_justification.bug_regression: 7.0/10 — 4 fixes verified. BUG-L-07 (z-fighting cauldron) still open — no cauldronGroup in LairProceduralGroups. Two latent bugs documented.
score_justification.code_quality: 8.0/10 — Consistent fallback pattern (.catch), interface-driven proceduralGroups, clear comments per GLB. Deducted 2.0 for missing material.clone() on bougie clones and missing cauldron hide path.
score_justification.performance_budget: 9.0/10 — 432 polys total, 120.52 KB bundle, 0 TS errors. Headroom confirmed.

next_steps.task: Fix BUG-L-SCALE-01: implement tiled mur_pierre approach (6×1 grid back, 5×1 sides) to preserve 1:1 stone aspect ratio. Zero Blender work required.
Fix BUG-L-07: add cauldronGroup to LairProceduralGroups and hide it in cauldron .then() callback. Pattern already established for mapGroup/shelfGroup.
Apply lore sublabels from immersion_recommendations.zone_sublabels (4 string swaps in main.ts lines 186-189). Score impact: +1.5 on immersion_lore.
Flag BUG-L-10 (bougie material.clone) as pre-condition on T-BOUGIE-FLICKER backlog item. No immediate action needed.
